attributes.content:
# Boxel Card Catalog

This is the overview document for the Boxel card catalog. The detailed card stubs now live in one document per category so the catalog stays smaller and easier to maintain.

## Catalog Principles

- Capture the domain memory an AI should not have to reconstruct from chat.
- Describe what makes the card distinct, reusable, and worth preserving.
- Skip obvious field lists until a card is ready for `.gts` implementation.
- Add more bullets only when lifecycle, provenance, or platform hooks are genuinely important.

## Condensed Stub Format

```md
### Card Name
**Priority:** P0 | P1 | P2 | P3

- **Core idea:** What this card uniquely owns or captures.
- **Must preserve:** Non-obvious state or provenance an AI must not lose.
- **Core loop:** The repeatable flow from creation to completion or revision.
- **System hooks:** Important commands, integrations, or platform constraints.
- **Extension seam:** What subclasses/providers can vary without changing the identity.
- **Success test:** What becomes possible once this card exists.
```

Only include the bullets that matter. For straightforward cards, a single `Core idea` bullet is enough.

## Category Documents

- [AI & Automation](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/ai-automation-catalog) (6 cards)
- [Communication](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/communication-catalog) (4 cards)
- [Commerce & Business](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/commerce-business-catalog) (12 cards)
- [Finance & Banking](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/finance-banking-catalog) (6 cards)
- [Projects & Productivity](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/projects-productivity-catalog) (7 cards)
- [Documents & Content](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/documents-content-catalog) (12 cards)
- [Design & Creativity](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/design-creativity-catalog) (11 cards)
- [People & Contacts](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/people-contacts-catalog) (11 cards)
- [Health & Wellness](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/health-wellness-catalog) (6 cards)
- [Travel & Logistics](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/travel-logistics-catalog) (5 cards)
- [Career & HR](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/career-hr-catalog) (15 cards)
- [Identity & Security](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/identity-security-catalog) (4 cards)
- [Learning & Education](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/learning-education-catalog) (12 cards)
- [Developer & DevOps](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/developer-devops-catalog) (11 cards)
- [Analytics & Data](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/analytics-data-catalog) (7 cards)
- [Legal & Compliance](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/legal-compliance-catalog) (15 cards)
- [Reports](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/reports-catalog) (5 cards)
- [Operations](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/operations-catalog) (2 cards)
- [Home & Personal](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/home-personal-catalog) (11 cards)
- [Standards & Guidelines](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/standards-guidelines-catalog) (4 cards)
- [Media Production](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/media-production-catalog) (5 cards)
- [Other](https://realms-staging.stack.cards/ctse/correct-cuckoo/Document/other-catalog) (5 cards)

## Reference Examples

- `AI Image` and `Workflow Playbook` in the `AI & Automation` document show the richer stub style for cards with meaningful provenance, lifecycle, and extension hooks.

## How to Use This Catalog

1. Start in the category document that matches the domain.
2. Keep the stub focused on identity, durable memory, and repeatable behavior.
3. Add schema, display details, and commands only when implementation begins.
4. Split or deepen a stub only when the card has non-obvious lifecycle or platform constraints.
